channel.title: Photo Feeds
channel.description: Combined feed of recent photo essays from The Atlantic, The Guardian, The Boston Globe, NYT, and Reuters.

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive for a visit to Penguin Beach to celebrate the 200th anniversary of the Zoological Society of London at London zoo in Regent’s Park. — Photograph: Jonathan Brady/PA

item.title: [Reuters — The Wider Image] Cape Verde Academy Soccer Talents Dream Big After World Cup Debut
item.description:
Youth trainees pose for a group photo after a football training session inside the Bola Pra Frente football academy in Praia, Cape Verde, June 30, 2026. REUTERS/Sodiq Adelakun

Source: Reuters — The Wider Image

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] Photos from June 2026: World Cup, high school championships and the return of summer
item.description:
BC High’s Christopher Morris carried the Championship trophy to the fans in the stands after defeating the Lincoln-Sudbury Warriors 10-9 in the MIAA Division 1 boys' lacrosse final at Worcester State on June 12. Barry Chin/Globe Staff

Source: Boston Globe — The Big Picture

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] Photos from June 2026: World Cup, high school championships and the return of summer
item.description:
A family member jumped up and cheered for her cousin as she walked across the stage and received her diploma during Chelsea High School Graduation in Chelsea, June 12. Jessica Rinaldi/Globe Staff

Source: Boston Globe — The Big Picture

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] Photos from June 2026: World Cup, high school championships and the return of summer
item.description:
Tick biologist Patrick Roden-Reynolds organized ticks on a lint roller after using tick drag in the backyard of a home during a tick survey in Aquinnah on May 18. Martha’s Vineyard is facing an extraordinarily high level of tick-borne disease, with the lone star being notable for carrying Alpha-gal syndrome. Finn Gomez for The Boston Globe

Source: Boston Globe — The Big Picture

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] Photos from June 2026: World Cup, high school championships and the return of summer
item.description:
The sun rose at Quincy Quarries Reservation in Quincy on June 3. Christian Kantosky for The Boston Globe

Source: Boston Globe — The Big Picture

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] Photos from June 2026: World Cup, high school championships and the return of summer
item.description:
Yan Morais, 7, of Dorchester dipped a buttercup into the water at The Arnold Arboretum in Jamaica Plain on June 7. Jessica Rinaldi/Globe Staff

Source: Boston Globe — The Big Picture

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] Photos from June 2026: World Cup, high school championships and the return of summer
item.description:
English High School’s Luis Mejia hoisted the trophy and cheered as he and his teammates celebrate their win over Hopedale at the conclusion of their Division 5 state quarterfinal at Boston English High School in Jamaica Plain on June 7. Jessica Rinaldi/Globe Staff

Source: Boston Globe — The Big Picture

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] Photos from June 2026: World Cup, high school championships and the return of summer

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] Photos from June 2026: World Cup, high school championships and the return of summer
item.description:

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] Photos from June 2026: World Cup, high school championships and the return of summer
item.description:
Danny Khattar, 15, a percussionist in the Boston Higashi School Jazz Band, scans the crowd as students and families fill the school gymnasium before the dress rehearsal of the Higashi School Winter Music Festival in Randolph in December. At this concert, Danny will sing lead vocals in the band's rendition of "Hot Chocolate." All 185 Higashi students perform at the festival.

Source: Boston Globe — The Big Picture

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] Photos from June 2026: World Cup, high school championships and the return of summer
item.description:
Family members are overcome with grief as they comforted one another at a prayer service held at the Islamic Society of Boston Cultural Center on June 27, for Mabinty Janneh, who was killed by a motorist who hijacked a car and hit her on a sidewalk in Mattapan. John Tlumacki/Globe Staff

Source: Boston Globe — The Big Picture

item.description:

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Chris McGrath / Getty — Emergency-service personnel work to repair the roof of the Dormition Cathedral of the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra after it was burned and damaged by Russian strikes on June 16, 2026, in Kyiv, Ukraine.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Tetiana Dzhafarova / AFP / Getty — A woman kneels next to a makeshift memorial near a residential building that was damaged during a Russian air attack on July 3, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
lina Smutko / Reuters — Iryna Plekhova, a resident who survived the previous day’s Russian missile strikes, carries some of her damaged personal belongings, including a Ukrainian coat of arms carved out of wood by her father-in-law, from her burned apartment on July 3, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Paula Bronstein / Getty — People watch as emergency workers clear debris from the scene of an overnight Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv that killed at least 14 people, on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Valentyn Ogirenko / Reuters — Rescuers work at the site of a damaged apartment building in Kyiv on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Danylo Antoniuk / AP — Liudmyla Tsapkova sits in her damaged apartment after a Russian missile attack in Kyiv on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Danylo Antoniuk / AP — Emergency-services personnel work to extinguish a fire following Russian missile attacks in Kyiv on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Valentyn Ogirenko / Reuters — Firefighters work to extinguish a fire at the Dormition Cathedral of the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, which was hit during Russian missile and drone strikes on June 15, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Marianna Kotyk / Reuters — Volunteers collect glass shards in a building at the Oleksandr Dovzhenko National Film Studio in Kyiv on June 19, 2026. The studio sustained significant damage during a large-scale missile and drone strike several days earlier. The costume shop, which housed the largest and oldest costume collection in Ukraine, was heavily affected.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Libkos / Getty — An aerial view shows the scale of destruction, including burned-down houses and damaged streets, in Vyshneve, on the outskirts of Kyiv, following a Russian night attack on July 6, 2026. The attack caused secondary detonations for more than three hours, destroying or heavily damaging hundreds of homes and forcing hundreds of residents to evacuate.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Gleb Garanich / Reuters — An explosion over the city, seen during a Russian missile and drone strike, on July 6, 2026

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Serhii Okunev / AFP / Getty — People shelter in a Kyiv metro station during a Russian missile strike on June 15, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Evgeniy Maloletka / AP — A Ukrainian rocket intercepts a Russian ballistic missile during a Russian attack on Kyiv on June 25, 2026. Recent shortages of U.S.-made interceptors have allowed more Russian ballistic missiles to get through, striking their targets.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Valentyn Ogirenko / Reuters — A woman cries while holding a child near the site of a damaged apartment building on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Efrem Lukatsky / AP — People look at the site of a Russian missile strike that hit a residential building on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Roman Pilipey / AFP / Getty — A person walks next a residential building damaged from a Russian air attack on the Ukrainian capital on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Kostiantyn Liberov / Libkos / Getty — Rescue workers operate at a destroyed section of a residential building after an explosion caused by a Russian strike on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Valentyn Ogirenko / Reuters — Rescuers carry a woman who was found under debris, inside an apartment building that was hit during overnight missile and drone strikes in Kyiv on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Kostiantyn Liberov / Libkos / Getty — A panoramic view of Kyiv shows smoke from a large fire spreading over the city following an overnight Russian missile and drone attack on July 2, 2026. Russia’s overnight attack on the capital killed at least 31 people and injured 100 others.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Efrem Lukatsky / AP — A woman carries her cat out of a damaged multistory apartment building following a Russian missile attack in Kyiv on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Danylo Antoniuk / AP — A woman looks up at a burning apartment building after a Russian missile attack in Kyiv, Ukraine, on July 2, 2026. Russian missiles have hit sites across the country, but Kyiv’s infrastructure and residential areas are especially frequent targets.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Pamplona revellers and a Nato summit: photos of the day – Tuesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Havana, Cuba — A man looks at his phone in Havana during a nationwide blackout on Monday night. Cuba, which suffered its third nationwide power outage since the start of the year, was already struggling to keep the lights on before Trump imposed an oil blockade in January, which has depleted the already dwindling supply of fuel for Cuba’s power plants. — Photograph: Yamil Lage/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Pamplona revellers and a Nato summit: photos of the day – Tuesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Pamplona, Spain — People take part in the San Fermin festival’s traditional encierro (bull run). The bull-running fiesta is held annually from 6 to 14 July in commemoration of the city’s patron saint. Visitors from around the world attend the festival, and many participate in the highlight event, in which they attempt to outrun the bulls along a route through the narrow streets of the old city. — Photograph: Rubén Albarrán/ZUMA Press Wire/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Berlin, Germany — Police use water cannon to spray tourists at the Brandenburg Gate during extreme hear in Berlin. All-time temperature records were broken in Germany, Poland, Slovakia, Czechia and Hungary, as well as June records in England and Switzerland, while France’s average temperature measured across the country also hit an absolute high. — Photograph: Krisztian Bocsi/Bloomberg/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Nicosia, Cyprus — A woman uses an umbrella to protect herself from the sun during a hot day. The consequences of extreme heat have been felt across Europe. More than two-thirds of Europeans experienced temperatures of more than 35C during the June heatwave. — Photograph: Petros Karadjias/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — Sofia Kenin of the US makes a return to her compatriot Amanda Anisimova at Wimbledon. Anisimova fired 20 aces past Kenin to reach the third round. With every win, she erases some of the memory of last year’s final, when she was beaten 6-0, 6-0 by Iga Swiatek. — Photograph: Toby Melville/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — Rufus the Harris’s hawk stands on the fist of falconer Donna Davis on the third day of the 2026 Wimbledon Championships. — Photograph: Henry Nicholls/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Atlanta, Georgia, US — England’s Harry Kane celebrates with Jude Bellingham after the first of his two goals during the World Cup match against Democratic Republic of Congo at Atlanta Stadium. Thomas Tuchel’s mission to put a second World Cup star on the England shirt did not look as though it would reach the second knockout round until Kane came to the rescue with two late goals. — Photograph: Ian MacNicol/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Miami, Florida, US — An Argentinian fan wearing a goat costume in Miami on the eve of the World Cup match between Argentina and Cape Verde. ‘Sharing the pitch with Messi is a dream,’ Cape Verde’s goalkeeper Vozinha said after his team’s draw with Saudi Arabia. ‘And I’ll proudly tell my kids I played against him one day’. — Photograph: Patrícia de Melo Moreira/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Medora, North Dakota, US — Donald Trump arrives by ceremonial train for the dedication of the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library. The sound of YMCA by the Village People booming through the badlands of North Dakota could only mean one thing: Donald Trump’s 250th anniversary travelling circus had reached a remote corner of America more familiar with bison, wild horses and bighorn sheep. — Photograph: Evan Vucci/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Beijing, China — People take photographs in front of a large flag of the Communist Party of China on the 105th anniversary of the founding of the organisation in Beijing. — Photograph: Kevin Frayer/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Monaco — The entrance of a residential building in Monaco where a suspected parcel bomb explosion wounded the Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Iermolaiev and his wife and child. An international search is under way for a suspected bomber in the unprecedented attack that has shaken the normally ultra-safe principality. — Photograph: Sébastien Nogier/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Durban, South Africa — A woman reacts as March and March protesters taunt foreigners allegedly living in a high-rise building. — Photograph: Marco Longari/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Verulam, South Africa — Supporters of the March and March movement at a demonstration against undocumented migrants in South Africa. The country was holding its breath on Tuesday as mass anti-immigration protests were held across the country. They came after a weeks-long campaign that has seen at least four killed and tens of thousands fleeing for safety. — Photograph: Marco Longari/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Guaira, Venezuela — A building damaged during the earthquakes in La Guaira. The official death toll so far is 2,595, but with 400 bodies reportedly being delivered to La Guaira’s morgue each day, that figure is certain to rise. — Photograph: Matias Delacroix/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Catia La Mar, Venezuela — Rescue workers search through the rubble of a building that collapsed when earthquakes struck Catia La Mar. A 43-year-old security guard was pulled from the collapsed basement of a shopping centre eight days after the earthquakes, to huge cheers from international rescue teams. — Photograph: Matias Delacroix/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Caracas, Venezuela — People look at buildings damaged after a magnitude 7.2 earthquake struck Venezuela in Caracas. More than 58,000 buildings may have been damaged and destroyed by the twin earthquakes that hit the country last week, according to a preliminary analysis of satellite data that suggests the scale of the destruction could dwarf official estimates. — Photograph: Jesús Vargas/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Israel — An Israeli soldier works on a tank near the border with Lebanon in northern Israel. Australian federal police investigators are assessing a brief detailing alleged war crimes committed by an Australian citizen serving in the Israel Defense Forces in Gaza. The allegations include the deliberate targeting and destruction of residential buildings without military justification, a war crime under Australian law. — Photograph: Ariel Schalit/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza — Palestinian children stand among destroyed homes in Gaza. — Photograph: Majdi Fathi/NurPhoto/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — Girls sit in a children’s playground. Russia regularly launches combined missile and drone attacks on the Ukrainian capital and there had been speculation for some days that another massive attack was coming. Volodymyr Zelenskyy had warned: ‘I am asking all our people to be extra careful, take care of yourselves and your children, and use shelters, this is very important’. — Photograph: Roman Pilipey/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — Iryna Plekhova looks for her remaining belongings in her burned-out apartment. As dawn broke after the overnight attack, fires were burning across Kyiv, with strikes or debris hitting residential buildings in several districts and a hotel on one of the central boulevards. — Photograph: Alina Smutko/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — A large fire burns across Kyiv after an overnight Russian attack. Russia used nearly 500 drones and more than 70 missiles in the hours-long attack on Kyiv and other parts of the country in the early hours of Thursday. — Photograph: Kostiantyn Liberov/Libkos/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — A woman holding a child cries next to a residential building damaged after a Russian air attack on Kyiv. At least 27 people were killed and dozens injured in what the city’s mayor, Vitali Klitschko, called the worst Russian attack on the capital during more than four years of air assault on Ukraine. — Photograph: Roman Pilipey/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Peter Kneffel / DPA / Getty — A crow flies past an oversize statue of a lion lying on its back and holding a beer mug, which is waiting to be installed on the grounds of the Theresienwiese for Oktoberfest in Munich, Germany, on June 30, 2026. The 191st Oktoberfest will take place from September 19 to October 4, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Cesar Manso / AFP / Getty — Revelers douse one another with red wine during the Batalla del Vino (“Battle of Wine”) in Haro, in the wine-producing region of La Rioja, Spain, on June 29, 2026. Every year thousands of locals and tourists climb a mountain in the northern Spanish wine-producing region to celebrate Saint Peter’s Day by soaking one another with red wine using water pistols, buckets, or any other available container.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Özgen Beşli / Anadolu / Getty — An insect alights on a wildflower blooming in a high-altitude area in Kars, Turkey, on July 1, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Lindsey Wasson / AP — A person calling themselves “Seattle Cloud Guy” jogs by during the Pride Parade in Seattle, Washington, on June 28, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Mike Egerton / PA Images / Reuters — Naomi Osaka wears a traditionally themed on-court outfit ahead of her match against Elsa Jacquemot during the ladies’ singles on Day 1 of the 2026 Wimbledon Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club, in London, on June 29, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
CFoto / Future Publishing / Getty — A dancer dressed in the attire of the Tang Dynasty, using a wire rigging, flies over the Wenfeng Pagoda and scatters peony petals in Luoyang City, Henan Province, China, on June 27, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Devi Rahman / AFP / Getty — A participant is briefly lifted into the air as a giant dragon-train kite catches the wind during a kite festival in Bantul, Yogyakarta, Indonesia, on June 28, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Charlie Riedel / AP — Fans celebrate during a watch party for the World Cup match between Team USA and the national team of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the KC Live! entertainment district in Kansas City, Missouri, on July 1, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Mohamed Elshahed / Anadolu / Getty — Children with physical and intellectual disabilities receive treatment and recognition at the Future Children Center in Cairo, Egypt, pictured on June 30, 2026. The center provides physical therapy and rehabilitation services tailored to each child’s individual needs, aiming to improve motor skills, enhance mobility, and support their integration into daily life.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Tierney L. Cross / Getty — A visitor views the U.S. Capitol behind the 110-foot Freedom 250 Ferris wheel on the third day of the Great American State Fair on the National Mall, pictured from the Washington Monument, on June 27, 2026, in Washington, D.C.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Alina Smutko / Reuters — People take shelter inside a metro station during an overnight Russian missile-and-drone strike in Kyiv, Ukraine, on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Valentyn Ogirenko / Reuters — Yana Hrynko, senior researcher of The National Museum of the History of Ukraine in the Second World War, shows bird’s nests made partially with fragments of fiber-optic lines that were found by a Ukrainian serviceman on the front line and then passed to the museum, in Kyiv, Ukraine, on June 23, 2026. Both Ukrainian and Russian troops use drones controlled via long lines of optic fiber to bypass electronic warfare jamming, leaving miles of ultra-thin lines tangled in trees and scattered across the land in Ukraine’s frontline regions.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Yasser Al-Zayyat / AFP / Getty — Lesser crested tern chicks flock together on Kubbar Island, off the southern coast of Kuwait, on June 27, 2026, during the annual breeding season, when thousands of seabirds nest on the island.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Ricardo Arduengo / Reuters — Yohancy Gil and her husband, Sergio Guanipa, stand on ruins as they wait for news from rescue teams looking for their children under the rubble at the site of a collapsed building in the aftermath of earthquakes, in La Guaira, Venezuela, on June 30, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Andreea Alexandru / AP — A couple walks by a public fountain on an extremely hot day in Bucharest, Romania, on June 27, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Michal Čížek / AFP / Getty — A woman cools off in the Vltava River in Český Krumlov, South Bohemia, Czech Republic, on June 27, 2026, as record-setting high temperatures hit the region during a heat wave.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Marton Monus / Reuters — A drone view shows people cooling themselves in a pool during a heat wave in Budapest, Hungary, on June 27, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Ertuğrul Baştan / Anadolu / Getty — People walk along a coastal promenade in the Ganita area during a sunset in the Ortahisar district of Trabzon, Turkey, on June 27, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Matias Baglietto / Anadolu / Getty — People gather at Parque Avellaneda for the annual Luz de Fuego celebration, centered on the burning of the Gran Fantoche de las Miserias (“The Great Puppet of Miseries”), in Buenos Aires, Argentina, on June 27, 2026. The traditional San Pedro and San Pablo Bonfire celebration features the burning of a massive handcrafted structure, built by local residents, symbolizing the collective release of personal and social hardships. Participants create their own symbolic puppets to cast into the flames, representing struggles such as war, hunger, violence, unemployment, and discrimination. Rooted in the cultural traditions of San Juan Night, the ritual combines fire, music, and community performances in a collective act of remembrance, renewal, and hope for a better future.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Michael Ciaglo / Getty — A man walks down the road after being turned back from going to his home as the Aspen Acres fire burns on July 1, 2026, in Pueblo, Colorado. As of yesterday, more than 35,000 acres and 100 structures had burned in the fire, one of several blazes that are currently burning across the state after a warm and dry winter.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Sakis Mitrolidis / AFP / Getty — A Greece Air Force Canadair CL-415GR water-bomber aircraft drops water over a forest fire in Liti, a village north of Thessaloniki, on June 30, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
McKlein Photography / LAT Images / Getty — Oliver Solberg competes in his Toyota GR Yaris Rally1 during Acropolis Rally Greece, Round 8 of the 2026 FIA World Rally Championship, on June 26, 2026, in Loutraki, Greece.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Yasuyoshi Chiba / AFP / Getty — A jockey balances on a wooden plow adapted for a race, attempting to steer two bulls through a flooded paddy field during Pacu Jawi, a traditional bull-racing event, in Simabur village, Tanah Datar Regency, West Sumatra, Indonesia, on June 27, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Julian Finney / FIFA / Getty — Erling Haaland of Norway celebrates after a 2–1 win during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 match between Côte d’Ivoire and Norway at Dallas Stadium on June 30, 2026, in Arlington, Texas.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Jordan Tovin / Reuters — An advertisement featuring Lamine Yamal of Spain’s World Cup national team gets towed along the East River past the Brooklyn Bridge on June 26, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Tom Nicholson / Reuters — People hang from a bridge above the Canal Saint-Martin after public swimming was permitted in certain areas because of high temperatures during a heat wave in Paris, France, on June 26, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Ethan Miller / Getty — A 300-foot-wide by 150-foot-tall American flag is suspended across the Hoover Dam on June 30, 2026, in the Lake Mead National Recreation Area in Nevada. The 2,000-pound flag and surrounding canyon walls are being illuminated by a lighting display nightly at dusk, through July 4, 2026, to mark the 250th anniversary of the United States’ Declaration of Independence.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: World Cup, Wine Battle, White Kimono
item.description:
Chip Somodevilla / Getty — A rainbow arcs over the giant, crumbling concrete busts of former U.S. presidents, including George Washington, at the Presidents Heads, following an evening rain storm on June 27, 2026, in Williamsburg, Virginia. Forty-three presidents, from George Washington to George W. Bush, are represented as 15-foot-tall, 11-ton concrete busts that were once part of Presidents Park, a 10-acre theme park in Williamsburg that closed in 2010 because of financial troubles. Sculpted by the artist David Adickes of Houston, Texas, the busts were rescued from demolition by the local builder Howard Hankins and stored on his private property. Plans are under way to relocate the busts to a nearby location for display and preservation.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

Baltal, India — The annual Amarnath Yatra, one of Hinduism’s largest pilgrimages, gets underway in the high-altitude Kashmir Himalayas. — Photograph: Mehran Firdous/Jna Press/ Nexpher/Zuma Press Wire/Shutterstock

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Messi dogs and a Himalayan pilgrimage: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Tehran, Iran — Mourners gather around the coffin of the late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei during a ceremony before his funeral at the Imam Khomeini Hosseiniyeh. — Photograph: Iranian supreme leader’s website/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

Kyiv, Ukraine — A resident returns to her home to look for personal belongings after surviving a Russian strike that burned down her apartment. — Photograph: Alina Smutko/Reuters

item.description:
London, UK — A photo of nine-year-old Ellerie Carroll from Yorkshire is placed next to a pair of shoes, part of the Walk in Our Shoes event. Hundreds of children’s shoes, each representing a child gone too soon, have been laid to mark National Bereaved Parents Day in Parliament Square. — Photograph: Jonathan Brady/PA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
